categories.name: Public Safety
categories.tldr: Keeps our neighborhoods safe -- police patrols, fire trucks, and making sure buildings follow safety rules.

lineItems.name: Sheriff Contract
lineItems.description: Contract with the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department for law enforcement services including patrol, traffic enforcement, and investigations.
lineItems.tldr: Pays for sheriff deputies who patrol Cerritos streets and respond to 911 calls.

lineItems.name: Fire Services
lineItems.description: Contract with the Los Angeles County Fire Department for fire suppression, emergency medical services, and hazardous materials response.
lineItems.tldr: Funds firefighters and paramedics who respond to fires and medical emergencies.

lineItems.name: Code Enforcement
lineItems.description: Ensures compliance with municipal codes including property maintenance, zoning, and nuisance abatement.
lineItems.tldr: Makes sure properties are maintained and neighborhoods stay clean and orderly.

categories.name: Parks & Recreation
categories.tldr: Keeps our parks green, runs youth sports and after-school programs, and plants trees throughout the city.

lineItems.name: Park Maintenance
lineItems.description: Maintenance of 23 city parks including landscaping, irrigation, playground equipment, and athletic fields.
lineItems.tldr: Takes care of all 23 city parks so they stay safe and beautiful for everyone.

lineItems.name: Recreation Programs
lineItems.description: Youth and adult recreation programs including sports leagues, classes, camps, and senior activities at the Cerritos Senior Center.
lineItems.tldr: Runs sports leagues, art classes, summer camps, and programs for all ages.

lineItems.name: Tree Planting & Care
lineItems.description: Urban forestry program including new tree plantings, pruning, removal of hazardous trees, and maintenance of the city's 25,000+ public trees.
lineItems.tldr: Plants new trees and cares for the 25,000+ trees lining our streets and parks.

lineItems.name: Heritage Park Maintenance
lineItems.description: Specialized maintenance for Heritage Park, including the sculpture garden, amphitheater, and community center facilities.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps Heritage Park's sculpture garden, stage, and community center in top shape.

categories.name: Public Works
categories.tldr: Fixes roads, picks up trash and recycling, runs the water system, and maintains city buildings.

lineItems.name: Street Maintenance
lineItems.description: Resurfacing, pothole repair, sidewalk maintenance, street lighting, and traffic signal operations across 115 miles of city streets.
lineItems.tldr: Repairs potholes, repaves roads, and keeps streetlights and traffic signals working.

lineItems.name: Waste Services
lineItems.description: Residential and commercial waste collection, recycling programs, organic waste diversion, and compliance with SB 1383 requirements.
lineItems.tldr: Handles trash pickup, recycling bins, and composting to keep waste out of landfills.

lineItems.name: Water System Operations
lineItems.description: Operation and maintenance of the city's water distribution infrastructure including pump stations, reservoirs, and 130 miles of water mains.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps clean water flowing through 130 miles of pipes to every home and business.

lineItems.name: Facilities Maintenance
lineItems.description: Maintenance and repair of city-owned buildings including City Hall, libraries, community centers, and the Cerritos Center for the Performing Arts.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps City Hall, libraries, and community centers running smoothly.

categories.name: Community Development
categories.tldr: Plans how the city grows, checks that buildings are safe, and attracts new businesses and jobs.

lineItems.name: Planning & Zoning
lineItems.description: Long-range planning, zoning administration, environmental review, and processing of development applications.
lineItems.tldr: Decides where homes, stores, and parks can be built and makes sure new projects fit the neighborhood.

lineItems.name: Building Inspection
lineItems.description: Plan review, building permit issuance, and field inspection services to ensure structures meet California Building Code standards.
lineItems.tldr: Checks that new buildings and renovations are safe and follow the rules.

lineItems.name: Economic Development
lineItems.description: Business attraction and retention, commercial corridor revitalization, and support for the Cerritos Auto Square and Cerritos Towne Center.
lineItems.tldr: Works to bring new shops and jobs to Cerritos and support local businesses.

categories.name: Administration & Finance
categories.tldr: Runs the behind-the-scenes stuff -- managing the city, tracking every dollar, and keeping tech running.

lineItems.name: City Administration
lineItems.description: City Manager's office, City Clerk, City Attorney, and Human Resources providing executive leadership and administrative support.
lineItems.tldr: The City Manager's team that oversees everything and keeps the city government organized.

lineItems.name: Finance
lineItems.description: Financial management, accounting, payroll, purchasing, and annual budget preparation and monitoring.
lineItems.tldr: Tracks every dollar the city earns and spends to make sure money is used wisely.

lineItems.name: IT Services
lineItems.description: Information technology infrastructure, cybersecurity, application support, and digital government services.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps the city's computers, networks, and online services running for staff and residents.

categories.name: Water Utility
categories.tldr: Cleans and delivers safe drinking water, maintains pipes and wells, and runs programs to help everyone save water.

lineItems.name: Water Treatment
lineItems.description: Operation of water treatment facilities, water quality testing, regulatory compliance, and source water protection.
lineItems.tldr: Treats and tests water to make sure every drop from your tap is safe to drink.

lineItems.name: Distribution System
lineItems.description: Maintenance and replacement of water mains, service lines, valves, hydrants, and metering equipment.
lineItems.tldr: Maintains the underground network of pipes and fire hydrants that deliver water everywhere.

lineItems.name: Conservation Programs
lineItems.description: Water conservation outreach, rebate programs for water-efficient fixtures, turf replacement incentives, and drought response planning.
lineItems.tldr: Helps residents save water with rebates for efficient fixtures and replacing thirsty lawns.
